acceptance_test: Ch12 contains no 'November' references. All seasonal/month references in Ch12 say December.
evidence: Ch12 line 3206: 'the thin remainder of the November afternoon'; Ch6 line 1188: 'December light'; Ch9 line 2129: 'three weeks' at the Manor; Ch10 line 2476: 'December dark'; Ch11 line 2795: 'grey December light'
problem: Ch12 line 3206 says 'November afternoon' when all surrounding chapters (Ch6, Ch9, Ch10, Ch11) have already established that the story has moved into December. November is inconsistent with the timeline.
rewrite_direction: Change 'November afternoon' at line 3206 to 'December afternoon.'

acceptance_test: Ch16, Ch17, and Ch18 all reference the same month. Duration claims ('six weeks of proximity') are arithmetically consistent with the chosen month.
evidence: Ch16 lines 4212, 4262, 4346, 4392: four 'January' references; Ch17 lines 4411, 4789: 'December' references; Ch18 lines 4804, 4830, 4974, 5046, 5136: five 'December' references; Ch18 line 4986: 'six weeks of proximity'
problem: Ch16 places the confrontation in January (4 explicit references), but Ch17-18 (the immediate aftermath) both say December. 'Six weeks of proximity' in Ch18 is consistent with December, not January. The novel must decide: either Ch16's January is wrong, or Ch17-18's December is wrong.
rewrite_direction: Change Ch16's four 'January' references to 'December' to align with Ch17-18 and the 'six weeks' arithmetic. Alternatively, change Ch17-18's December references to January, but this requires also adjusting the 'six weeks' duration claim.

acceptance_test: Ch7 places the Dark Mark on Draco's left forearm, consistent with all other chapters.
evidence: Ch7 line 1735: 'His left hand went to his right forearm — the Mark'; Ch2 line 272: 'He pushed up his left sleeve'; Ch3 line 401: 'his left sleeve rolled to the elbow'; Ch6 line 1192: 'He rolled up his left sleeve'; Ch8 line 1798: 'rolled his left sleeve to the elbow'; Ch11 line 2791: 'roll up his left sleeve'
problem: Ch7 line 1735 places the Dark Mark on Draco's RIGHT forearm ('His left hand went to his right forearm — the Mark'). Every other reference in the novel (Ch2, Ch3, Ch6, Ch8, Ch11, Ch12, Ch15) and the continuity sheet place it on his LEFT forearm.
rewrite_direction: Change 'His left hand went to his right forearm' to 'His right hand went to his left forearm' at line 1735.

acceptance_test: The folio's title is identical in Ch11 and Ch16, and if its location has changed, the house's role in moving it is acknowledged.
evidence: Ch11 line 2905: folio found in 'main library, not the gated archive,' shelf 14-C; Ch11 line 2921: title 'On the Binding Architecture of the Manor and Its Proper Maintenance for the Perpetuation of the Line'; Ch16 line 4076: folio found in 'gated archive, third unit from east wall'; Ch16 line 4080: title 'On the Residential Bonding Architecture of Malfoy Manor, Its Purposes, Constraints, and Conditions of Activation'; Ch16 line 4112: 'It was the same folio'
problem: Hermione finds the bonding folio in Ch11 in the main library with one title. Draco finds it in Ch16 in the gated archive with a different title. The narration at line 4112 asserts it is 'the same folio' despite different locations and different titles. If the house moved it, this should be stated. If they are different documents, line 4112 is wrong.
rewrite_direction: Reconcile the location and title. Either: (a) make the titles identical and add a sentence noting the house moved the folio into the archive, or (b) make them clearly two different documents and remove the 'same folio' assertion at line 4112.

acceptance_test: Ch8 contains no 'November' references that describe the present-day timeline. Flashback references to the past are clearly marked as past.
evidence: Ch6 line 1188: 'December light'; Ch8 lines 1898: 'November, not July. Twenty-five, not sixteen'; Ch8 line 1976: 'a November evening at half four'
problem: Ch8 says 'November' at lines 1898 and 1976 when Ch6 (two chapters earlier) has already established that the season has moved to December. Line 1898 is a grounding statement after a flashback ('November, not July') that should say 'December, not July.'
rewrite_direction: Change 'November' to 'December' at lines 1898 and 1976 in Ch8.
